title: OpenAI Agents SDK Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for using the OpenAI Agents SDK effectively.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content where variability is desired.

commands.description: Limits the number of tokens in the generated response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on token usage.

commands.description: Uses the GPT-4o model with streaming enabled for real-time output.
commands.scenario: When building a live chat application that requires immediate responses.

commands.description: Generates multiple response variations for each input.
commands.scenario: When testing different outputs to select the best one.

commands.description: Defines a stopping sequence to end the generation.
commands.scenario: When you want to ensure the output stops at a specific point.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Returns the log probabilities of top tokens.
commands.scenario: When analyzing the model's confidence in its predictions.

commands.description: Discourages the model from repeating similar phrases.
commands.scenario: When you want diverse and varied output.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same lines verbatim.
commands.scenario: When generating long-form content to avoid redundancy.

commands.command: Use concise language and avoid jargon.
commands.description: Instructs the model to produce clear and simple output.
commands.scenario: When generating content for a general audience.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for the API request to avoid long waits.
commands.scenario: When integrating into systems with strict latency requirements.

commands.description: Specifies the API key for authentication with OpenAI services.
commands.scenario: When setting up the SDK for the first time.

commands.description: Ensures deterministic outputs by setting a fixed seed for reproducibility during testing.
Nucleus sampling: limits the model's consideration to the top tokens whose cumulative probability reaches P, reducing tail noise.
Monitor the system_fingerprint in responses to detect backend model updates that might affect output consistency.
Structure prompts with explicit reasoning steps in examples to improve complex logic handling.
Configure automatic retry logic for handling rate limits (429) or transient server errors (5xx).
Forces the agent to call a tool instead of responding with text, useful for strict function-calling workflows.
Programmatically prevents the model from generating specific words or tokens by applying a negative bias to their logit values.
Prompt the agent to generate a baseline response, then generate verification questions to check its own facts, and finally produce a revised response.
A debugging technique to dump the exact JSON schema being sent to the model to ensure tool definitions match the expected SDK structure.
Optimizes performance and cost by reusing large system prompts or static datasets across multiple agent turns to reduce input token processing.
